question: The concept of 'sustainable urban development'
options: Meeting the needs of urban populations without compromising future generations
Widens the valley as the river meanders across the floodplain
Low birth and death rates; stable or slowly growing population
Environment offers possibilities and constraints but humans choose their response
answer: Meeting the needs of urban populations without compromising future generations

question: 'Hydraulic action' in coastal erosion
options: Waves compress air in rock cracks, eventually breaking rock apart
A sand bar connects an island to the mainland through deposition
Meeting the needs of urban populations without compromising future generations
Maximum population an environment can sustain without degradation
answer: Waves compress air in rock cracks, eventually breaking rock apart

question: The concept of 'bid-rent theory' (Alonso)
options: Maximum population an environment can sustain without degradation
Low birth and death rates; stable or slowly growing population
A rocky peak protruding above an ice sheet
Land value decreases with distance from the CBD; different users outbid each other
answer: Land value decreases with distance from the CBD; different users outbid each other

question: A 'fjard' differs from a fjord because
options: A sand bar connects an island to the mainland through deposition
Meeting the needs of urban populations without compromising future generations
Rapid urbanisation outpaces provision of formal housing in developing nations
It is shallower and less steep-sided, formed in lowland glaciated coastlines
answer: It is shallower and less steep-sided, formed in lowland glaciated coastlines

question: The concept of 'environmental determinism'
options: Physical environment determines human culture and behaviour — largely discredited
Downward cutting of the river bed deepening the valley
Low birth and death rates; stable or slowly growing population
It is shallower and less steep-sided, formed in lowland glaciated coastlines
answer: Physical environment determines human culture and behaviour — largely discredited

question: 'Possibilism' in geography argues
options: Environment offers possibilities and constraints but humans choose their response
Meeting the needs of urban populations without compromising future generations
A rocky peak protruding above an ice sheet
It is shallower and less steep-sided, formed in lowland glaciated coastlines
answer: Environment offers possibilities and constraints but humans choose their response

question: The concept of 'the demographic transition model stage 4'
options: A rocky peak protruding above an ice sheet
Low birth and death rates; stable or slowly growing population
Waves compress air in rock cracks, eventually breaking rock apart
Downward cutting of the river bed deepening the valley
answer: Low birth and death rates; stable or slowly growing population

question: A 'tombolo' forms when
options: Rapid urbanisation outpaces provision of formal housing in developing nations
A sand bar connects an island to the mainland through deposition
The transition zone between built-up urban areas and the countryside
Waves compress air in rock cracks, eventually breaking rock apart
answer: A sand bar connects an island to the mainland through deposition

options: Waves compress air in rock cracks, eventually breaking rock apart
Low birth and death rates; stable or slowly growing population

answer: The transition zone between built-up urban areas and the countryside

The transition zone between built-up urban areas and the countryside

options: The transition zone between built-up urban areas and the countryside
Low birth and death rates; stable or slowly growing population

Population movement from city centres to outer suburbs

options: A sand bar connects an island to the mainland through deposition
Widens the valley as the river meanders across the floodplain
A rocky peak protruding above an ice sheet
Waves compress air in rock cracks, eventually breaking rock apart
answer: A rocky peak protruding above an ice sheet

options: Waves compress air in rock cracks, eventually breaking rock apart
Population movement from city centres to outer suburbs
Land value decreases with distance from the CBD; different users outbid each other
Maximum population an environment can sustain without degradation
answer: Maximum population an environment can sustain without degradation

options: Downward cutting of the river bed deepening the valley
Low birth and death rates; stable or slowly growing population
Population movement from city centres to outer suburbs

options: Physical environment determines human culture and behaviour — largely discredited
